description_long: This is a traditional East Asian pagoda structure with a broad, gently sloping green-tiled roof and ornate, upturned eaves. The roof is supported by a series of intricately detailed wooden beams and columns, with decorative gold and brown accents along the edges and under the eaves. The building sits atop a raised stone platform with a central staircase leading to a prominent entrance, flanked by symmetrical sets of steps and railings. The walls feature a grid of wooden latticework and paneling, giving the structure a refined and historic appearance. The roof's ridges are adorned with subtle ornamental details, but there is no prominent spire or finial at the apex. The overall form is compact and single-storied, with a strong sense of symmetry and balance typical of Japanese temple architecture.
description: A single-story East Asian pagoda with a green-tiled, upturned roof, ornate wooden details, and a raised stone platform entrance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, central staircase, and symmetrical roof eaves.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended width of the roof and the detailed wooden latticework along the walls.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with visible steps and ornate roof structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the broad roof and wall paneling.
